criteria_verification.criterion_text: `derive_request_id(*, build_id: str, stage_label: str, attempt_count: int) -> str` is a pure function — keyword-only, no I/O, no hidden state
criteria_verification.evidence: Signature uses leading bare `*` to make all three parameters keyword-only. Body contains only argument validation, two calls to a pure helper that wraps urllib.parse.quote, and an f-string return — no file I/O, no network, no globals mutated, no datetime/uuid/random. TestSignatureIsKeywordOnlyAndPure asserts the kind of every parameter is KEYWORD_ONLY via inspect.signature and verifies positional invocation raises TypeError.
criteria_verification.notes: Signature uses leading bare `*` to make all three parameters keyword-only. Body contains only argument validation, two calls to a pure helper that wraps urllib.parse.quote, and an f-string return — no file I/O, no network, no globals mutated, no datetime/uuid/random. TestSignatureIsKeywordOnlyAndPure asserts the kind of every parameter is KEYWORD_ONLY via inspect.signature and verifies positional invocation raises TypeError.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Same inputs produce identical output across calls (property test)
criteria_verification.evidence: TestSameInputsProduceIdenticalOutput::test_repeated_calls_agree iterates 5x per parameter set on 7 representative inputs. test_property_sweep_is_idempotent runs a 120-combination sweep (6 build_ids x 4 stage_labels x 5 counts) and asserts each pair agrees. Acts as a hypothesis-style property test without adding a new dependency.
criteria_verification.notes: TestSameInputsProduceIdenticalOutput::test_repeated_calls_agree iterates 5x per parameter set on 7 representative inputs. test_property_sweep_is_idempotent runs a 120-combination sweep (6 build_ids x 4 stage_labels x 5 counts) and asserts each pair agrees. Acts as a hypothesis-style property test without adding a new dependency.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Different `attempt_count` values produce different output (so refresh-on-timeout per `API §7` produces a distinguishable id)
criteria_verification.evidence: TestAttemptCountChangesOutput::test_consecutive_attempt_counts_differ generates a set comprehension over attempt_count in range(50) holding build_id+stage_label fixed and asserts len == 50 (no collisions). test_pairwise_distinct adds parametrized pairwise checks across non-adjacent values (0-1, 1-2, 5-10, 0-100, 12-13).
criteria_verification.notes: TestAttemptCountChangesOutput::test_consecutive_attempt_counts_differ generates a set comprehension over attempt_count in range(50) holding build_id+stage_label fixed and asserts len == 50 (no collisions). test_pairwise_distinct adds parametrized pairwise checks across non-adjacent values (0-1, 1-2, 5-10, 0-100, 12-13).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Output format is stable, documented, and URL-safe (no characters that would break NATS subject parsing if ever embedded)
criteria_verification.evidence: Module docstring documents the wire-contract format f'{enc(build_id)}:{enc(stage_label)}:{attempt_count}' and the alphabet restriction. _encode_component runs urllib.parse.quote(safe='') and post-encodes '.' and '~' so the output cannot contain '.', '~', '*', '>', or whitespace. TestOutputIsUrlSafeAndNatsSafe::test_stable_documented_format pins the exact string for the canonical example. test_output_alphabet_is_restricted regex-validates output against ^[A-Za-z0-9_\-:%]+$ across 6 inputs including unicode, dots, slashes, and wildcards. test_no_nats_subject_breakers explicitly probes inputs containing every NATS-forbidden char and asserts none leak. test_separator_layout pins the colon-separator positioning.
criteria_verification.notes: Module docstring documents the wire-contract format f'{enc(build_id)}:{enc(stage_label)}:{attempt_count}' and the alphabet restriction. _encode_component runs urllib.parse.quote(safe='') and post-encodes '.' and '~' so the output cannot contain '.', '~', '*', '>', or whitespace. TestOutputIsUrlSafeAndNatsSafe::test_stable_documented_format pins the exact string for the canonical example. test_output_alphabet_is_restricted regex-validates output against ^[A-Za-z0-9_\-:%]+$ across 6 inputs including unicode, dots, slashes, and wildcards. test_no_nats_subject_breakers explicitly probes inputs containing every NATS-forbidden char and asserts none leak. test_separator_layout pins the colon-separator positioning.

criteria_verification.evidence: Implementation: `if attempt_count < 0: raise ValueError(...)`. TestNegativeAttemptCountRaises::test_negative_value_rejected parametrized over -1, -2, -100, and -2**31. test_zero_is_accepted pins the lower-boundary positive case (0 is the natural first-attempt value).
criteria_verification.notes: Implementation: `if attempt_count < 0: raise ValueError(...)`. TestNegativeAttemptCountRaises::test_negative_value_rejected parametrized over -1, -2, -100, and -2**31. test_zero_is_accepted pins the lower-boundary positive case (0 is the natural first-attempt value).

criteria_verification.evidence: Implementation: two leading `if not <arg>: raise ValueError(...)` guards before encoding. TestEmptyComponentsRaise covers empty build_id, empty stage_label, and both empty.

criteria_verification.evidence: identity.py imports only `from __future__ import annotations` and `from urllib.parse import quote`. TestModuleImportsAreDomainPure parses the module source with ast.parse and walks ast.Import / ast.ImportFrom nodes to assert the forbidden set {'nats_core', 'nats', 'langgraph'} is disjoint from the actual import set. AST-based check correctly ignores docstrings/comments that mention the forbidden names while explaining their absence.
